Extended material - Figure 1Consensus clustering of cancer cell - ConsensusClusterPlus results

SignalMin.max BasalImmunogenicProliferativeEMT
Cancer cell clustersExtended material - Figure 2

bExtended material - Figure 3
a
a Scatter plot displaying the distortion (sum of squared errors) according to the number of k-means clusters. The cells in the dataset were clustered according to their proportion of neighboring cells in a radius of 30 micrometers. b Hierarchical clustering heatmap of the proportion of cell populations per cell neighbor cluster (recurrent cellular neighbors). The cell neighbors were estimated using different radii and then grouped in 30 clusters using k-means. e Heatmap displaying the correlation of RCN abundances across the tumors. Only pairwise significant (p<0.05) correlations are shown.

ReceiverTumorSenderFOXP3_CD4_TregTumorExtended material - Figure 4
The Multinichenetr predicted ligand-receptor interactions distinguishing high and low MHCII tumor expression in scRNA-seq dataset. Circosplots displayligand–receptor pairs prioritized byMultinichenetr–bothimmune–tumorand tumor–tumor cellinteractions.

ng high and low MHCII tumor expression in scRNA-seq dataset. Circosplots displayligand–receptor pairs prioritized byMultinichenetr–bothimmune–tumorand tumor–tumor cellinteractions. Immunecells (CD8+ T cells, CD4+ T cells, FOXP3+ regulatory T cells, and dendritic cells (DC)) are shown in orange, while tumor cellsareshownin yellow.Eachplot displays the presence of ligand-receptor interactions, indicating whether they are associated with high or low cancer MHCII expression in the samples.
